onboarding.question: While specifying the onboarding engineering spec, I found that AC#23 uses "after inactivity" without a numeric bound on how quickly the timer reset is reflected server-side. This requires the product spec to be revised to specify that the inactivity timer reset is applied server-side within one request-response cycle (within 500ms) of the triggering user action.
onboarding.recommendations:
1. My recommendation: the inactivity timer reset must be applied server-side within one request-response cycle of the triggering user action, with a maximum server-side propagation delay of 500ms.
→ Rationale: a user who sends a message or types in the prompt bar has a reasonable expectation that their session is immediately extended — any perceptible delay between action and reset creates a risk of unexpected expiry and erodes trust in the logged-out experience.

AC#23 should be updated to read: "Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer; the timer reset is applied server-side within one request-response cycle, with a maximum server-side propagation delay of 500ms."

I'll apply that now.
